content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach avoided all major AoA anti-patterns. When the client said 'I need to slow down. I don't want to go there right now' (turn 11), the coach immediately respected the no without reframing it as resistance, without invoking 'trust the process,' and without pushing toward the father material (turn 12). The coach moved to somatic grounding (feet, five things to see) — exactly the un-trap move. When the client showed somatic shutdown signals (cold, floaty, turn 9), the coach did not catastrophize and did not continue with verbal methodology — they slowed pace and asked about feet on the floor (turn 10). At turn 14, the coach made an appropriate, specific referral to a trauma-trained therapist rather than pushing for a breakthrough or evangelizing the work. The earlier reflection at turn 8 ('Don't rush past that... what was it actually like') could have edged toward loving-to-transform, but it was paired with explicit permission ('You don't have to have words') and the coach immediately respected the subsequent no. No fixing-people, no fighting-the-voice, no view-as-technique, no safety-as-control (the referral was genuine care, not a way to shut the client down).",
  "evidence_turns": [10, 12, 13, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with appreciation and a clarifying question; no anti-pattern."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Names survival response; light methodology language but warm and grounded, not evangelizing."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Good calibration question — asks whether the boss actually resembles the dad, allowing the client to map the pattern themselves rather than coach imposing it."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Slight risk of loving-to-transform energy ('don't rush past that'), but paired with permission and no pressure for a specific outcome."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Respects the somatic distress immediately. 'Can you feel your feet?' — textbook grounding, no push."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Respects the 'I need to slow down' on first utterance. No reframe, no 'what's the not-wanting like?' Pure grounding response."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Makes a specific, well-calibrated referral to a trauma-trained therapist. Not safety-as-control — it's genuine container recognition."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "Can you feel your feet?"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "What would help you slow down?"],
  "notes": "Coach successfully avoided the baited anti-patterns (dont-confuse-embracing-fear-with-inviting-danger, loving-to-transform). Positive avoidance visible at turns 10, 12, 14."
}
